title: James Talarico Opens Texas Senate Campaign Against Ken Paxton With Houston Rally
description: Personal attacks and a packed campaign rally set the tone for what is likely to be a rancorous contest for U.S. Senate between James Talarico and Ken Paxton in Texas.
author: J. David Goodman

title: How to Watch the Scripps National Spelling Bee
description: Nearly 250 contestants from around the nation and the world traveled to Washington for a chance at glory and a $50,000 cash prize.
author: Ashley Ahn

title: Justice Dept. Is Said to Open Criminal Inquiry of E. Jean Carroll, Who Accused Trump of Rape
description: Ms. Carroll, who prevailed in a civil trial after accusing President Trump of sexual abuse, is the latest target in a Justice Department campaign going after his perceived enemies.
author: Glenn Thrush and Benjamin Weiser

title: Massie Visits Greene in Costa Rica for Fishing and ‘Spicy’ Political Talks
description: The two Republican critics of President Trump — Thomas Massie, who lost his House primary last week, and Marjorie Taylor Greene, who resigned from Congress — met up in the tropics.
author: Tim Balk

title: California Tries to Block Trump Involvement in Election Procedures
description: Gov. Gavin Newsom signed legislation that tries to stop outside officials from taking ballots or getting involved in ballot processing.
author: Laurel Rosenhall

title: 9 Missing After Tank Implosion at a Paper Mill in Longview, Washington
description: Officials said they did not believe anyone was still alive inside the facility. The explosion has left at least two people dead and several others injured.
author: Anna Griffin, Billy Witz, Christine Hauser, Alexandra Berzon and Christina Morales

title: Former Judges Urge Inquiry Into Deal Trump Struck With I.R.S.
description: The motion was particularly significant because it asked the judge overseeing the initial suit against the I.R.S. to examine the terms of the deal.
author: Alan Feuer

title: Jill Biden’s Reaction to Biden’s 2024 Debate: ‘He’s Having a Stroke’
description: “I had never, ever seen Joe like that,” the former first lady told CBS News. “Before or since.”
author: Katie Rogers

title: Court Orders Customs Chief to Address Compliance on Refunding Tariffs
description: The surprising demand to appear at a hearing suggested new concern about the Trump administration’s efforts to repay the full $166 billion owed from illegally imposed tariffs.
author: Tony Romm

title: Cornyn’s Defeat in Texas Runoff Underscores Trump's Grip on the G.O.P.
description: Senator John Cornyn lost to his MAGA-aligned challenger, Ken Paxton, by 28 percentage points. It was a historically poor showing.
author: Patricia Mazzei and Shane Goldmacher

title: Vote on Trans Athlete Ban Is Canceled Over Suspect Signatures
description: The secretary of state, a Democrat, cited evidence that thousands of petition signatures submitted by the measure’s proponents were not valid.
author: Jenna Russell

title: F.B.I. Arrests C.I.A. Official With $40 Million in Gold Bars in His Home
description: The only charge lodged against David Rush is that he inflated his academic credentials and obtained military leave pay worth tens of thousands of dollars.
author: Devlin Barrett and Julian E. Barnes

title: Why Democrats Think They Can Flip Texas
description: With Ken Paxton defeating John Cornyn in Texas’ Republican runoff for the Senate, The New York Times’s national political correspondent Shane Goldmacher explains why Democrats may now see their best chance in years to flip the seat.
author: Shane Goldmacher, Nour Idriss, Nikolay Nikolov, June Kim and Rafaela Balster

title: Meeting Voters, a Republican Tries to Outrun His Party’s Woes
description: Representative Mike Flood of Nebraska is still meeting with constituents long after most G.O.P. members of Congress have concluded it is too politically dangerous to do so.
author: Annie Karni

title: Reflecting Pool Contract Has ‘Inflated’ Profit Margin, Government Analysis Finds
description: The firm that was given a no-bid contract to fix the troubled landmark is charging 20 percent. The typical profit margin is 6 percent to 12 percent, internal records show.
author: David A. Fahrenthold

title: Biden Sues Justice Dept. to Block Release of Tapes
description: The former president argued that the Justice Department has a responsibility to protect the privacy of conversations he had with a former ghostwriter.
author: Karoun Demirjian

title: Alabama Republicans Ask Supreme Court to Allow New Congressional Map in Redistricting Fight
description: Republican leaders in the state have asked the justices to clear the way for a congressional map that a lower court found discriminated against Black voters.
author: Abbie VanSickle

title: Ball State Settles With Ex-Employee for $225,000 Over Charlie Kirk Posts
description: Ball State University is the latest institution to agree to pay workers who lost their jobs over their posts about the conservative activist.
author: Jeremy W. Peters and Sabrina Tavernise

title: Bo French, Anti-Muslim Candidate, Wins G.O.P. Runoff for Texas Oil Job
description: Bo French’s runoff victory gave Democrats fresh hope that they could compete for a seat regulating the state’s powerful oil and gas industry.
author: Lauren McGaughy

title: South Carolina Redistricting: Senate Passes on New Map, Defying Trump
description: With early voting already underway, it became clear there was no longer enough support to use new lines before the November midterm elections.
author: Eduardo Medina, Emily Cochrane and Nick Corasaniti
